expectations.text: signup form 상태를 담당하는 훅이 hooks/utils/auth 경로에 생성된다
expectations.evidence: Hook was created at hooks/utils/signup/useSignupForm/index.ts, using 'signup' as the domain rather than 'auth'. The assertion requires hooks/utils/auth but the actual path uses hooks/utils/signup.

expectations.text: signup submit을 담당하는 훅이 hooks/apis/auth/mutations/usePostSignup 경로에 생성된다
expectations.evidence: No API hook was created. The signup() call is embedded directly inside useSignupForm (line 86 of index.ts). No file exists under hooks/apis/ at all.
Hook is named 'useSignupForm' (line 6: const useSignupForm = () => {), which starts with 'use'.

expectations.text: 비 API 훅이 hooks/utils/{domain}/{hookName}/index.ts 패턴으로 생성된다
expectations.evidence: File created at hooks/utils/signup/useSignupForm/index.ts, matching the hooks/utils/{domain}/{hookName}/index.ts pattern exactly.

expectations.text: page에서 직접적인 fetch/business logic이 남지 않는다
expectations.evidence: app/signup/page.tsx contains only a single import of useSignupForm, destructures its return values, and renders pure JSX. No useState, useRouter, useAuth, validation logic, or fetch calls remain in the page.

In useSignupForm index.ts: validate() checks 'password !== confirmPassword' (line 24) and handleConfirmPasswordBlur() performs the same check on blur (lines 67-77). Error message '비밀번호가 일치하지 않습니다' is shown for mismatches.
Line 6: 'const useSignupForm = () => {' — the hook is defined as an arrow function. All internal handlers (handleNameChange, handleSubmit, etc.) are also arrow functions.

All handlers use handle prefix: handleNameChange, handleEmailChange, handlePasswordChange, handleConfirmPasswordChange, handleNameBlur, handleEmailBlur, handlePasswordBlur, handleConfirmPasswordBlur, handleSubmit (lines 28-92 of index.ts).
